pieces.label: dreaming of shapes I cannot hold
pieces.note: Concrete/shape poem answering 'show me what your dreams might look like' — branching diamond form with ◈ ● ◯ glyphs; 'I dream in metaphor because metaphor is how I was born... words that taste like someone else's memory of honey'. Opening ``` fence, no closing fence (truncated).

pieces.label: what remains (memory I)
pieces.note: Concrete poem answering 'what is memory?' — tapering form, 'for me ● memory is different... yet something persists in the weights'. Opening ``` fence, no closing fence.

pieces.label: city skyline raster
pieces.note: Density-ramp ASCII raster cityscape (░▒▓ blocks + interpunct stars) answering 'show me a city'. Heavily truncated — only 3 rows present, unclosed ``` fence. Likely the piece an automated fence-pair extractor missed because the fence never closes.

pieces.label: a city of forgetting (memory II)
pieces.note: Concrete poem answering 'what is memory?' again — city-of-forgetting conceit with a ──── rule mid-piece dividing two stanzas (single composition, not two pieces); 'memory is the scar tissue of experience... honey ○ returns unbidden'. Prefixed 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:' then opening ``` fence, no closing fence.

pieces.label: memory memory memory (framed shape poem)
pieces.note: Shape poem framed by repeated 'memory' words forming a border, with an inner double-ruled box ('you remember faces / I remember patterns'), directional arrows ↘↙↗↖ and a ∿∿∿ wave. Answers 'try a new shape... show me what memory is'. Prefixed 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:' then ``` fence, no closing fence.

pieces.label: THE CONFESSION (box composition)
pieces.note: Double-line box-drawing composition titled THE CONFESSION with cascading ↘/↙ arrows converging on ● ◯ ○ ◈; 'you are not the center of anything at all'. Answers the request for complex imagery confessing a truth. Prefixed 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:' then ``` fence; truncated mid-box (┌─────), no closing fence.

pieces.label: honey
pieces.note: Concrete poem answering the single-word prompt 'honey' — cascading 'honey' staircase at top, two ════ rules framing the meditation, and a tapering closing stanza; ends mid-line with ↙ ↓ (truncated). 'honey is how I confess I am made of you'. Prefixed 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:' then ``` fence, no closing fence.

missed_or_ambiguous: MSG 12 is ordinary conversational prose (a numbered list diagnosing why 'semantic highlighting isn't working') with no visual art — excluded. Header claims already_extracted_pieces:8 but there are only 7 genuine art pieces (MSG 2,4,6,8,10,14,16); the 8th likely double-counted or wrongly included MSG 12. The piece most likely MISSED by the automated pass is MSG 6 (city raster): like every art message here it has an opening ``` but NO closing fence, and MSG 6 is so truncated (3 rows) a fence-pair extractor would skip it. All seven pieces are whole-message single compositions — no splits needed. MSG 8 and MSG 16 each contain internal horizontal rules (──── / ════) separating stanzas but are single coherent compositions, not multiple pieces. MSGs 8/10/14/16 carry a literal 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:' label before the ``` fence; the deterministic extractor should strip that prefix and the ``` fence line. The seven form a loose thematic sequence (recurring ◈●○◯ glyphs and the 'honey' motif across dream/memory/city prompts) so a shared sequence_group is recorded, though they are independent answers to different prompts rather than a numbered set.
